item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one still gives me chills every time I read it. Absolutely effective horror that mostly happens in the reader's head rather than in anything explicit on the page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The fetus reminded me of the fetus from P.T.

+1

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with the following image, which is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0):

Title: 1782-1.png
Author(s): Department of Conservation, faminepulse
Release year: 2020
Note: Created by faminepulse using the image below.

Source: Flickr
License: CC BY 2.0
Title: Stephen Bragg with kakapo chicks
Author: Department of Conservation

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had me at abortion, my friend.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think Kojima is an SCP fan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
blood and feces

173 does look kind of like a fetus

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this one but where did all the images go?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this SCP. It gives a large amount of mindf—ks, yet still be pretty clear on what is going in here, without telling you out right. It could be a bit more subtle to me though, like I would write "Fetus in the sink" instead of "Abortion in the sink." Though that's just a minor nitpick.

However, I feel that the twist of the SCP is an aborted reality warper isn't supposed to be the main thing of this article, I think that it's supposed to make you feel dread, horror, and uncomfortableness; the tone feels like a silent hill game and I love it.

Room appears with two partitions, including a bathroom housing only a toilet, and a small rectangular entrance accessible through a small hole in the wall.

Well that probably explains it.

anyways, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the reality bending (in terms of the general spookiness/weirdness) is par for the course, but the circumstances elevate this into upvote territory.

+1
congratulations Freudian!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've come back to this years after first reading it and it's still utterly, tremendously, completely terrifying and sad in equal measures. I think it has changed a bit since I first read it to more explicitly explain the origins of the phenomena, but that's not at all to its disadvantage. It is now and probably forever my favourite SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One of the few SCPs that can actually make me feel uncomfortable anymore. And that's a good thing. A damn good thing.

Upvoted, dear sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoiler Theory
Not sure about this, Bob...

I don't think the abortion is SCP-239. According to the author, it's Daniel from SCP-2782.

Also the location and circumstances don't match SCP-239s acquisition logs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: after reading the comments and confirming my suspicions i have no choice but to +1 this scp

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Heh, this one is fantastic simply because the description of events is exactly like a dream, down to all the exceptionally weird bits like the pop song and the guy feeding himself to birds. Especially the friggin' pop song. That sort of zany shit is exactly what folks always forget to include when attempting to describe nightmares in fiction. :P

[Okay, so Faminepulse confirmed the SCP's exactly based on a dream officially, but I got that vibe before reading that comment, so there's that. :P ]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…Oh. Oh. OH.

That's alright, I didn't need anymore sleep this year anyways.

Take my +1 while I get a freaking drink.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Different strokes for different folks, as they say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoiler response
– hide block

That doesn't really help in terms of my own personal enjoyment. If anything, it makes it worse, because it kills the mystery

item.title: Original Image
item.content:encoded: http://media-cache-ec0.pinimg.com/236x/67/e5/4e/67e54e175cc529d0e6a4a18a8876048d.jpg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
thing is, the spoopy occurrences aren't disparate and random; they have a unifying cause. It's extremely subtle and difficult to understand, but once you do get it and use it to link together all the events together, it's damn terrifying.

+In case you can't figure it out
-Le spoilers

The fetus in the toilet was an aborted reality bender, specifically SCP-239. It's the thing causing all of this shit to happen. Come to your own conclusions as to how each event is related to the entity; that's what boosted this skip up to my favorites.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The YeIIow Duck mirrors my sentiments exactly. It's a random-shifting room with random spoopies happening. The more I turn it over in my head, the more random it seems, and the less scary it seems, like watching the same horror movie over and over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is legitimately one of the most godawful weirdest and creepiest things I've ever read. Incredibly well written, with excellent use of pictures. I probably wont sleep tonight. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP is the only one that has kept me awake at night in terror… and I've read a shitton of SCPs. Due to that, this is my new all tine favorite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank ye much. I hope I can write another that does that for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sound does not travel through the interior

Like… if you clapped your hands in there, it wouldn't make noise.

temporarily reaching impossible speeds

The camera man would not be able to spin the camera around that fast.

and a small rectangular entrance accessible through a small hole in the wall

The room changes. The hole simply appeared, unless I'm reading your question wrong.

Also, SCP-1782 is in Ukraine, but the words imprinted are Slovak. By the way, do these numbers have any specific meaning?

I must have gotten the language wrong when I was researching it… 198d is a lyrically relevant At The Drive-In song because I'm cheesy like that.

uncased septic tank

An easier mental image would be a terribly rusty, grime stained bath tub.

Thanks very much for the translation. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It has been over a year since I first read this.

To this day, I still freak out a little bit any time I hear the words "hole in the wall".

Your goddamn upvote is probably the most well-earned one I have ever given.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great. Last time I remember myself shivering when making a translation, it was SCP-835.

But the wording is super awkward in some places.

Sound does not travel through the interior

Does that mean nothing can be heard, or does the air itself not transfer sounds?

Dr. Sanders' words seem really out of context.

and a small rectangular entrance accessible through a small hole in the wall

I thought the entrance replaced the usual door. Took me some time to understand it was not related to the door in any way.

temporarily reaching impossible speeds

As in FTL travel?

uncased septic tank

As far as I understand, a septic tank IS essentially a case. I have a hard time imagining that.

Also, SCP-1782 is in Ukraine, but the words imprinted are Slovak. By the way, do these numbers have any specific meaning?

item.title: imhc
item.content:encoded:
The old man is indirectly responsible for the creating the entity. It has something to do with his trying to increase the kakapo population. He is a scientist.

But not really…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is actually really terrifying to me, especially the old man matter-of-factly stating "There's an abortion under the floorboards. One in the sink, too".

Although I admit I did not understand the connection between the old man, abortion, cameraman, and hole at first.

Altogether, a really good read. The pictures (diagram and camera image of hole) add to the article - make it seem more real, and the whole thing is expertly-crafted and full of info (DNA reviews, interviews, etc.).

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
/reads the article

/gets confused but understands some of it

/reads the comments

/hops on the Nope Train to Fuck-That-Ville

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image out of context and its placement is to confuse the reader.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved it, only suggestion would be to place the picture with the birds farther down, so as to be next to what it depicts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I felt the same after I'd first read it through. I'd suggest reading the spoilers posted in the comment pages, they definitely helped make it a uniquely disturbing SCP for me.
On the other hand, I'm not totally sure I want to understand.

item.title: Whoa!!!
item.content:encoded:
The first SCP I ever read was 087. Linked on a creepypasta forum. It was terrifying, and it introduced me to the new and unsettling world of the foundation. It's been a while since then. And honestly, I've been looking at SCP at less a horror site and more just a site with of empirical reports of the paranormal. Not a bad thing, but after being exposed to the wide range of them, I found that I didn't look for horror as much as I was looking for weird, like when I watch the twilight zone.

But this…whoa…this brought me back to what a horror story could be in this format. Unsettling. Creepy. Subtle. All applied to the mixed media format of an scp (and you used them all lol: a photograph, a blueprint, a recording, an interview, a log). You even spiked it with a horror movie sequence while preserving the feel of an official document. All with a very cool story to boot. This needs to get to triple digits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish I could upvote comments.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I… Don't get it. I really don't.

So… It's a random room with some creepy stuff happening in it? And it's creepy in the most generic and bland way possible?

Sorry. I don't see any brilliance in it. I might be missing some symbolism or whatever. For now I'm not voting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Brilliant, brilliant, brilliant! Also, very disturbing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: likewise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
oh man.

Came here from Soulless' tale, and: Yeah, upvoted. This is done real well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's a hole in the wall in the bottom of the floor

There's a hole in the wall in the bottom of the floor

There's a hole

There's a hole

There's a hole in the wall in the bottom of the floor

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I edited it a bit. That better?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kid must have thought his mom was full of crap for trying to kill him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sometimes, SCPs don't necessarily need to make sense to be good. This is one of those times. I really don't need the explanation to find myself willing to upvote.

Possibly it is just for the line: "There's an abortion under the floorboards, one in the sink, too."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is utterly fucking brilliant.
Also, kudos for proper grammar slovak text :3
That said, though (rereading after the explanation, I didn't get it either) in the log of the camera panning.

Faint crying can be heard as the object's head moves slightly to the right with the aid of skeletal hands from opposing sides of the camera's view. Brown liquid sprays onto the object's face before video feed ends.

This bit is written in a super confusing manner, I think. I suspect the object is the same orange-jumpsuit entity, but why suddenly refer to it differently?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I honestly love the way this looks like an array of random crap at first, but then most everything comes together into coherence.

I like the idea of an exploration log that was created by the SCP itself for the purpose of showing off: "look. look at my scary".

And this line

Must've been all of that sugar and brandy I drank before she brought me in here.

just plain keeps itself lodged in my head. As if he'd been pulled out of some "Rent-A-Creepy Manifestation" repository on the astral plane. Hey, it's a living.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is like 239 minus a couple years.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's better than Poopstick McGee.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're welcome. Maybe "symbolism" wasn't the right word, but however, what I meant is that one can either read it simply as a "weird room with weird stuff" or go a little deeper and see that there's a story behind it.

…Unless I'm mistaken. But that's the feeling I got.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey thanks.

I don't know about all this about symbolism though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has become one of my favorites. After a long time spent lurking around, when I read this one, I thought "Well now I just HAVE to become a member, because I need to upvote it."
I think it's perfect as it is, I'm perfectly fine with the SCP being obscure enough to have readers rack their own brains to understand it. And even without all the reality bending business and the symbolism, it would still be great.
I think adding some more area records would be nice. Not a lot of them, just one or two more, possibly. But it's not like the thing needs them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my. I thought I had most of the symbolism figured out by now, but now I feel dense because I had completely forgotten about the septic tank.
So you mean that they are… the same thing now? Care to explain, please?

item.title: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: This is going nowhere. Thread closed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Again, you're either missing the point of my posts totally or deliberately misinterpreting them. I'm writing this based off what was read in the discussion section? No shit, sherlock. That's the entire point of my suggestion. The reality bending explanation is spectacular and puts everything into perspective, but is evidently a really hard conclusion to come across, as the boatload of "I don't know what the fuck's going on but I like it" posts are demonstrating. If you disagree with this premise and think it's better left open to interpretation, then that's just a matter of personal taste. I'm of the opinion that the author's intepretation blows everything else out of the water and gives an awesome fucked up context to the SCP.

I've said before that I don't want to beat the reader over the head with some punchline that amounts to "It's a reality bender, dummy", but I think it would be helpful to drop something in to let them know that the reality bender concept is in play. No one thinks about reality benders because almost every SCP on the site bends reality in some way. You could explain everything with "A reality bender did it", and in most situations it's really unsatisfying, but here it makes perfect sense and works beautifully. The reader doesen't know this beforehand though, and will accept a room full of crazy anomalous shit with an abortion as a recurring theme, as a room full of crazy anomalous shit with an abortion as a recurring theme. This is cool, but if you know the underlying concept everything starts to make sense and it's horrible and so good.

Anyways, I'm not really impressed when someone's posts consist of passive aggressive invective and then they get all upset when I call them slow for missing the point. That just tells me you can dole it out but you can't take it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
and because we can only look back at the events through the distorted lens of a posthumous fetal reality bender, so everything has to come across as sympathetic.

The problem with what you're writing is that it is based off of what was read in the discussion section than what was actually written in the article. I think that the beauty of the article rests in its ambiguity rather than "FETUS!". If you really want sympathy from the reader, much of the article would have to be changed for your ideas to work.

From looking at the article, here's what I get: The room changes all the time, entities are created through unknown means, and GPSs are pinging from people's uterus-es. I would assume that reality is being altered in some way. The article ends with the story of trying to retrieve the fetus, so I would also assume that the fetus is doing the altering, or at least the fetus is the center of the craziness in the apartment. I don't need an aside where a doctor - If this thing could alter reality to this extent as a fetus, than I shiver to think of the consequences of what would have happened had it come to term… - Dr. Fuckwitit. We also have other reality benders that can bend reality better than this one, the only difference here is that this thing has no true personality because it's a FETUS. I guess that would be the reason this article was titled "Tabula Rasa" instead of "Unreality Baby".

Also, please don't start off a response with an insult to try to ramp up the "argument". This isn't YouTube, and throwing insults does not make your point more valid; it often detracts from what you're trying to get across.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"They never found the body?
Oh, they did… But it wasn't a body any more."
I read that line somewhere once. Can't remember where, but it seemed vaguely appropriate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What I got out of that one was that the fetus vanished the next time the room reset itself; the essence of the thing is still stuck inside of 1782, what was seen was just another manifestation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: if someone wrote a story about a character named "Albie Burgers," i'd probably want to kick the author in the nuts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
http://gbweekly.co.nz/2009/7/29/kakapo-care-challenges-and-delights-conservation-volunteers

If anyone is curious the man with the birds is named Albie Burgers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Beyond the ones listed above, the only thing that comes to mind is that it's kind of a mystery how the Foundation got alerted to an apartment in a condemned building in Kiev. Strange reality bends in the area would be a good way for them to zero in on it.. But… yeah, I don't know. I'd probably be fine if it stayed as it is right now, it's hard for me to describe how much I love this thing as it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're pretty dense, aren't you? You still haven't grasped the fact that the abortion isn't what I'm trying to clarify here, it's the reality bender part. The GOC guy isn't intended to bring to light the fact that a tragedy happened and get readers to nod their head and write a letter to their congressman, it's to draw attention to the fact that he's from the GOC, which wouldn't get involved in anything that wasn't paranormal in the first place. He's apologizing because he's both pleading for his pitiful little existence with something that's both immensely powerful and very pissed off, and because we can only look back at the events through the distorted lens of a posthumous fetal reality bender, so everything has to come across as sympathetic.
